reasoning: The Lean statement correctly encodes the informal claim. The collection `T_inf := {t | Set.Infinite tᶜ ∨ t = ∅ ∨ t = univ}` matches $\{U \mid X-U \text{ is infinite or empty or all of } X\}$, since $U^c = \emptyset \iff U = \text{univ}$ and $U^c = \text{univ} \iff U = \emptyset$. The three conjuncts (univ ∈ T_inf, closed under sUnion, closed under pairwise intersection) are exactly Mathlib's `TopologicalSpace` axioms. The phrase "does not need to be a topology" is naturally formalized as `∃ X, ¬(axioms hold)`, i.e., there is some X where T_inf fails to be a topology — and this is true (e.g., X = ℕ, where evens and odds-from-3 both have infinite complements but their union has finite non-empty complement {0,1}). Nothing is dropped, added, strengthened or weakened.
